paper.title: Efficient Transformers via Learned Sparse Attention

segments.speaker: A
segments.text: So this paper claims transformers can be made dramatically faster. Is that actually true?

segments.speaker: B
segments.text: Mostly, yes. The trick is to attend to a learned subset of tokens instead of all of them.

segments.speaker: A
segments.text: So instead of every word looking at every other word, the model picks a few important ones?

segments.speaker: B
segments.text: Exactly. Figure two shows the sparsity pattern the model discovers on its own during training.

segments.speaker: A
segments.text: And that grid is what saves all the computation?

segments.speaker: B
segments.text: Right. The cost drops from quadratic to almost linear, with barely any accuracy loss.

segments.speaker: A
segments.text: That is a genuinely great tradeoff. Thanks for breaking it down so clearly.
